rationale: The citing paper explicitly says it extends prior multifunctional-product work on feature complexity/usability and usage overestimation, and the cited paper is a key study on feature-rich vs. feature-poor preferences and feature fatigue via social utility. This is a direct conceptual overlap in the same phenomenon (multifeature product choice) and could be a substantive theory/mechanism source rather than a passing mention. The DOI match indicates a specific linked reference.
